task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI system control panel (setup screen), 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)' is selected as the data source.

task_completion.description: Configure the synthetic source for a 4-channel Ganglion board
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the setup screen, the board type is set to 'Ganglion' or the channel count is explicitly set to 4 channels.
The main OpenBCI GUI dashboard is active, showing various data widgets, indicating the session has been started.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Head Plot widget is visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Head Plot' widget is open and visible on the main dashboard.

task_completion.description: Remap the four channels to O1, O2, P3, and P4
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Head Plot widget or its settings, the 4 active channels are explicitly labeled or mapped to the 10-20 system locations O1, O2, P3, and P4.

task_completion.description: Verify the Head Plot visualization reflects posterior electrode positions
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Head Plot graphic (the top-down view of the head), the active/colored nodes are located in the posterior (back/bottom) region of the head model, corresponding to the occipital and parietal lobes.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine OpenBCI GUI application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual OpenBCI GUI interface, not a text editor, mockup, or web search.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration and session starting were done via the graphical interface, without relying on terminal commands to edit configuration files directly.

integrity.description: The session is genuinely running and streaming data
integrity.visual_evidence: Other widgets (like Time Series) show active waveforms or data streaming, proving the session was actually started and is running.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI Ganglion board natively supports exactly 4 channels. The target channels for this visual montage are O1, O2, P3, and P4, which correspond to the Occipital and Parietal lobes. In the Head Plot visualization, these electrodes should appear in the posterior (back/bottom) region of the head model.
